title:
Lesson 1:
Not Yet / On My Way
description: 'Henüz değil' or 'daha değil' = not yet. 'Geliyorum' = I'm coming. 'Hemen geliyorum' = I'm coming right now. These are essential phrases for everyday scheduling.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Come on, we're waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm coming, I'm coming. Give me two minutes.

title:
Lesson 2:
Leaving & Coming Back
description: 'Artık gidiyorum' = I'm leaving now. 'Döndüm' = I'm back (I returned, speaker). 'Döndü' = he/she is back. 'Artık' signals that something is happening now or has changed.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I'm leaving now. See you later.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Okay. What time will you be back?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I don't know. I'll let you know.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Evet, döndü. Ben de döndüm zaten.
dialogue.english: Yes, he's back. I'm already back too.

title:
Lesson 3:
Almost Done / Almost There
description: 'Neredeyse' means 'almost'. 'Az kaldı' = just a little left. 'Neredeyse geldim' = I'm almost there. 'Az kaldı bitireceğim' = I'm almost done. Very useful when someone is waiting.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Where are you? We're waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm almost there. Two minutes left.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Az kaldı bitireceğim. Biraz daha var.
dialogue.english: I'm almost done. Just a little more.
